issue_title: MCP SSE parsing returns the first event (a progress notification) instead of the JSON-RPC response, dropping tools/call results
issue_body: ### What happened? `McpHttpClient.parseResponse` ([McpHttpClient.kt:127-139](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/mcp/McpHttpClient.kt#L127-L139)) handles `Content-Type: text/event-stream` responses by joining every `data:` line in the body into one `\n`-separated string and then calling `JSONObject(eventData)` on that blob: ```kotlin private fun parseResponse(responseText: String): JSONObject { val trimmed = responseText.trim() if (trimmed.startsWith("{")) return JSONObject(trimmed) val eventData = trimmed .lineSequence() .filter { it.startsWith("data:") } .joinToString(separator = "\n") { it.removePrefix("data:").trim() } .trim() if (eventData.startsWith("{")) return JSONObject(eventData) error("MCP response did not contain a JSON object") } ``` This works for servers that return exactly one SSE event whose `data:` line is the JSON-RPC response. Real MCP servers routinely stream **multiple** events — a `notifications/progress` notification while a long-running tool call is in flight, then a final response event when the work completes. When that happens the code emits two concatenated JSON objects on consecutive lines: ``` {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress","params":{"progress":0.5}} {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"result":{"tools":[{"name":"echo"}]}} ``` `JSONObject(eventData)` is lenient and parses only the **first** object, then stops. The actual response — the one with the matching `id` and a `result` (or `error`) — is silently dropped on the floor. The caller in `request()` ([McpHttpClient.kt:97](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/mcp/McpHttpClient.kt#L97)) then runs ```kotlin return response.optJSONObject("result") ?: JSONObject() ``` against the notification object, which has no `result` key, and returns an empty `JSONObject`. Downstream consequences visible in the live build: - `listTools()` ([McpHttpClient.kt:37-51](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/mcp/McpHttpClient.kt#L37-L51)) reads `result.optJSONArray("tools")` from the empty object → falls back to an empty `JSONArray` → returns `emptyList()`. The MCP result card surfaces `No tools returned.` against any server that ever streams a progress event before responding. - `callTool(...)` ([McpHttpClient.kt:53-70](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/mcp/McpHttpClient.kt#L53-L70)) reads `result.optBoolean("isError", false)` (false) and `result.optJSONArray("content")` (null), so the user-visible **MCP result** card renders `{}` (the stringified empty `JSONObject`) and reports the call as **succeeded with no content**. The tool's real return value never reaches the agent. - Errors are also lost: a `notifications/progress` event followed by `{"id":1,"error":{"code":-32601,"message":"Method not found"}}` is reported as a "successful call with no content" instead of surfacing the JSON-RPC error. The bug is at the MCP transport boundary, completely separate from PR #140 (which trims `Mcp-Session-Id` header padding on a different line of the same file). There is no test file at all for `McpHttpClient` — `app/src/test/java/dev/touchpilot/app/mcp/` does not exist on `main`. ### Expected behavior `parseResponse` should parse each SSE `data:` line as its own JSON object and surface the **JSON-RPC response event** (the object that carries `id` together with `result` or `error`), not the first event in the stream. Concretely, for a server response with one or more `notifications/progress` events followed by a response event, `parseResponse(...)` MUST return the response event's object so that `request()` can hand a populated `result` (or surface the `error`) up to the caller. Pure single-event responses (`startsWith("{")` short-circuit, or one `data:` line) must continue to behave exactly as before; the public surface of `listTools`, `callTool`, and `initialize` must not change shape. ### Steps to reproduce Two complementary repros — a deterministic JVM unit test, and a live LDPlayer device repro against a tiny SSE mock server. #### A. JVM unit test repro (deterministic, no emulator required) 1. Temporarily widen the visibility of `parseResponse` (one keyword) so a unit test can call it directly. In `app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/mcp/McpHttpClient.kt`: ```diff - private fun parseResponse(responseText: String): JSONObject { + internal fun parseResponse(responseText: String): JSONObject { ``` 2. Add a temporary regression test at `app/src/test/java/dev/touchpilot/app/mcp/McpHttpClientSseProbe.kt`: ```kotlin package dev.touchpilot.app.mcp import kotlin.test.Test import kotlin.test.assertEquals import kotlin.test.assertTrue class McpHttpClientSseProbe { private val client = McpHttpClient("http://example.invalid/mcp") @Test fun progressNotificationFollowedByResultPicksResult() { val sse = """ event: progress data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress","params":{"progress":0.5}} event: message data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"result":{"tools":[{"name":"echo"}]}} """.trimIndent() val parsed = client.parseResponse(sse) assertEquals(7, parsed.optInt("id")) assertTrue(parsed.has("result")) } @Test fun multipleNotificationsThenResultPicksResult() { val sse = """ data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress","params":{"progress":0.25}} data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress","params":{"progress":0.75}} data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"result":{"ok":true}} """.trimIndent() val parsed = client.parseResponse(sse) assertEquals(3, parsed.optInt("id")) assertTrue(parsed.has("result")) } @Test fun errorResponseEventIsPickedOverPriorNotifications() { val sse = """ data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress","params":{"progress":1.0}} data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"error":{"code":-32601,"message":"Method not found"}} """.trimIndent() val parsed = client.parseResponse(sse) assertTrue(parsed.has("error")) assertEquals(-32601, parsed.optJSONObject("error")!!.optInt("code")) } } ``` 3. Run only the new test class: ``` .\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest --tests dev.touchpilot.app.mcp.McpHttpClientSseProbe ``` 4. Observe three failures on `main`: ``` McpHttpClientSseProbe > progressNotificationFollowedByResultPicksResult FAILED AssertionError: expected:<7> but was:<0> McpHttpClientSseProbe > multipleNotificationsThenResultPicksResult FAILED AssertionError: expected:<3> but was:<0> McpHttpClientSseProbe > errorResponseEventIsPickedOverPriorNotifications FAILED AssertionError: Expected value to be true. 3 tests completed, 3 failed ``` In every case `parseResponse` returned the *notification* object — `id` is `0` (absent on a notification, so `optInt` returns the default), and `result`/`error` are absent — instead of the response event. Delete the regression test file and revert the `private` → `internal` change once observed; this issue does not propose committing them. #### B. Live LDPlayer device repro (matches the on-device "no tools returned" symptom) Run a tiny SSE mock server on the host that streams `notifications/progress` before each response, point TouchPilot's **Settings → MCP** panel at it, then tap **List Tools** and **Call Tool**. 1. From a PowerShell terminal on the host machine, start the mock server (single command, listens on `http://localhost:8000/mcp`, returns a single event for `initialize` and a multi-event SSE for `tools/list` / `tools/call`): ```powershell $listener = New-Object System.Net.HttpListener $listener.Prefixes.Add('http://localhost:8000/') $listener.Start() while ($listener.IsListening) { $ctx = $listener.GetContext(); $req = $ctx.Request; $res = $ctx.Response $body = '' if ($req.HasEntityBody) { $reader = New-Object System.IO.StreamReader($req.InputStream, $req.ContentEncoding) $body = $reader.ReadToEnd() } $method = $null; $id = 1 if ($body -match '"method"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"') { $method = $matches[1] } if ($body -match '"id"\s*:\s*(\d+)') { $id = [int]$matches[1] } $res.ContentType = 'text/event-stream' $res.Headers.Add('Mcp-Session-Id','mock-session-1') $payload = switch -Regex ($method) { '^initialize$' { 'data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":' + $id + ',"result":{"protocolVersion":"2025-11-25","capabilities":{},"serverInfo":{"name":"BugDemoServer","version":"0.1.0"}}}' + "`n`n" } '^tools/list$' { "event: progress`ndata: " + '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress","params":{"progress":0.5}}' + "`n`n" + "event: message`ndata: " + '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":' + $id + ',"result":{"tools":[{"name":"echo","description":"Demo echo tool."},{"name":"weather","description":"Demo weather tool."}]}}' + "`n`n" } '^tools/call$' { "event: progress`ndata: " + '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress","params":{"progress":0.5}}' + "`n`n" + "event: message`ndata: " + '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":' + $id + ',"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello from mock echo"}]}}' + "`n`n" } default { 'data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":' + $id + ',"result":{}}' + "`n`n" } } $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($payload) $res.ContentLength64 = $bytes.Length $res.OutputStream.Write($bytes, 0, $bytes.Length) $res.OutputStream.Close(); $res.Close() } ``` 2. Bridge the LDPlayer instance's `localhost:8000` to the host's `localhost:8000` via `adb reverse`: ``` adb -s emulator-5554 reverse tcp:8000 tcp:8000 ``` 3. In TouchPilot, navigate to **Settings → MCP**. Type `http://localhost:8000/mcp` into the **Server** field. The MCP endpoint chip flips to **CONFIGURED**. 4. Tap **List Tools**. The mock server delivers two tools (`echo`, `weather`) in the response event — verified by hitting the same endpoint with `Invoke-RestMethod` on the host, which returns the full SSE body — but TouchPilot's `MCP result` card shows: ``` MCP initialized: { "protocolVersion": "2025-11-25", "capabilities": {}, "serverInfo": { "name": "BugDemoServer", "version": "0.1.0" } } Tools: No tools returned. ``` The initialize response (single event) parsed correctly. The `tools/list` response (two events) lost the `tools` array because `parseResponse` returned the notification's `JSONObject`, which has no `result.tools`. ![List Tools shows "No tools returned" despite the server sending two tools](bug7-list-tools-result.png) 5. Type `echo` into the **MCP tool name** field, then tap **Call Tool**. The mock server delivers `result.content = [{"type":"text","text":"hello from mock echo"}]` in the response event. TouchPilot's `MCP result` card shows: ``` MCP echo -> true {} ``` The call is reported as **succeeded** (because the notification's empty `result` has no `isError` key, so `optBoolean("isError", false)` returns `false`), but the body is the stringified empty object — the actual content was thrown away. ![Call Tool returns empty {} body despite the server sending content](bug7-call-tool-result.png) Both surface failures share the same root: `parseResponse` returned the wrong SSE event. If it had returned the response event, `listTools` would render the two-tool array and `callTool` would render `hello from mock echo`. Stop the mock server (Ctrl+C in its PowerShell window) and remove the `adb reverse` (`adb -s emulator-5554 reverse --remove tcp:8000`) once observed. ### Device or emulator - LDPlayer 9 (`D:\LDPlayer\LDPlayer9\dnplayer.exe`), instance index 0, 720×1440 @ 240 dpi, on Windows 10 Pro x64. - The JVM repro requires no emulator (`./gradlew testDebugUnitTest`). ### Android version Android 9 Pie, API level 28 (irrelevant for the JVM repro). ### TouchPilot commit `a0ba5b0e14670645a07e3467ae84da36d2c42af9` (current upstream `main` as of 2026-06-03). `parseResponse` is unchanged from the original implementation — no commit on `main` between `9884d46` and `a0ba5b0` touches `McpHttpClient.kt`. ### Accessibility service enabled? Yes (irrelevant for either repro). ### Areas involved - [ ] Android control - [ ] Agent loop - [ ] Local router / local inference - [ ] Skills - [x] MCP - [ ] Security / approvals - [ ] UI ### Logs, screenshots, or traces Two LDPlayer captures from the device repro above, both taken against a debug APK built from current `main` against the SSE mock server in step B1 above: - `bug-list-tools-result.png` — **List Tools** returns "No tools returned" even though the mock server emitted two tools (`echo`, `weather`). - `bug-call-tool-result.png` — **Call Tool** to `echo` returns `MCP echo -> true` and an empty `{}` body, even though the mock server emitted `content: [{"type":"text","text":"hello from mock echo"}]`. JVM probe output (deterministic, no emulator required): ``` McpHttpClientSseProbe > progressNotificationFollowedByResultPicksResult FAILED AssertionError: expected:<7> but was:<0> McpHttpClientSseProbe > multipleNotificationsThenResultPicksResult FAILED AssertionError: expected:<3> but was:<0> McpHttpClientSseProbe > errorResponseEventIsPickedOverPriorNotifications FAILED AssertionError: Expected value to be true. 3 tests completed, 3 failed ```
